reasoning_trace.signal: 2 full-price products at ₹1,450-2,200, avg rating 4.55, editorial placement, limited stock on one
reasoning_trace.proves: Premium demand exists at full price; trend validated at high price point
reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Whether demand will sustain at value-fashion price points; durability concerns not addressed
reasoning_trace.tension: Strong premium signal contrasts with weak Meesho presence and internal POS underperformance

reasoning_trace.signal: 2 products at ₹899-949 with 15-20% discount, rank 3 and 8, high review velocity, clean discount context
reasoning_trace.proves: Organized mass retail demand is strong and organic, not discount-driven
reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Whether demand is for Chanderi silk specifically or for the look/design; durability not tested
reasoning_trace.tension: Aligns with Nykaa on demand existence but at lower price; conflicts with internal POS failure

reasoning_trace.signal: 1 imitation product at ₹349, 1,800+ units sold, 45 resellers, 3.4 rating, slow growth
reasoning_trace.proves: Price-sensitive market is aware of Chanderi look but prefers cheaper imitation; real silk not moving
reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Whether a ₹699-899 real Chanderi would sell; imitation success may not translate
reasoning_trace.tension: Weak presence and imitation focus conflict with premium signals from Nykaa and Myntra

reasoning_trace.signal: Similar SKU underperformed: 38% sell-through, 22% margin, 18% return rate, metro-only success
reasoning_trace.proves: Chanderi silk has narrow appeal in our stores; durability complaints; metro-only viability
reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Whether a different price point or blended fabric would change outcome
reasoning_trace.tension: Directly contradicts Nykaa and Myntra signals; suggests premium demand may not translate to our base

reasoning_trace.key_tensions: Nykaa/Myntra show strong premium demand but internal POS shows failure with similar product; Meesho shows imitation success, not real silk
reasoning_trace.key_convergences: All sources agree Chanderi look has demand; disagreement is on material and price point viability

chanderi-straight.headline: ⚡ Sources disagree: Premium signals from Nykaa/Myntra clash with internal POS failure and Meesho imitation focus

conflicts.title: Nykaa/Myntra strong demand vs Internal POS underperformance
conflicts.nykaa_says: Strong premium demand at full price, editorial push
conflicts.myntra_says: Strong organic demand at ₹899-949 with low discount
conflicts.internal_says: Similar SKU failed: 38% sell-through, 18% returns, metro-only
conflicts.buyer_question: Will our value-fashion customer accept real Chanderi silk given past durability complaints?
conflicts.proves: Chanderi look has demand across channels; premium segment willing to pay full price
conflicts.cannot_prove: Whether our target customer will buy real Chanderi at ₹699-899 without durability issues

conflicts.title: Meesho imitation success vs Nykaa/Myntra real silk demand
conflicts.nykaa_says: Real Chanderi silk at ₹1,450-2,200 selling well
conflicts.myntra_says: Real Chanderi silk at ₹899-949 selling well
conflicts.meesho_says: Imitation Chanderi look at ₹349 selling 1,800+ units
conflicts.internal_says: Real Chanderi silk underperformed in value segment
conflicts.buyer_question: Is the demand for the Chanderi look or for real silk?
conflicts.proves: Chanderi aesthetic has broad appeal from premium to value
conflicts.cannot_prove: Whether real silk is necessary or a blended fabric would capture demand

convergences.title: Chanderi aesthetic has demand across price tiers
convergences.detail: Nykaa (premium), Myntra (mass premium), and Meesho (value) all show some form of Chanderi-related demand
convergences.proves: The look is trending; not a niche
convergences.cannot_prove: Optimal price point and fabric composition for our customer

convergences.title: Low discount distortion on Myntra/Ajio
convergences.detail: Both products are Strategic Value Pricing with low discounts, indicating genuine demand
convergences.proves: Demand is not artificially inflated by discounts
convergences.cannot_prove: Whether demand will persist at full price or with higher discounts

chanderi-straight.upside_summary: Chanderi silk straight kurti with zari border has strong premium demand signals from Nykaa and Myntra/Ajio, with near-zero discount distortion. The aesthetic is clearly trending across price tiers, including a Meesho imitation. If we can address durability concerns (e.g., blended fabric), there is potential to capture value-fashion demand at ₹699-899.
chanderi-straight.catch_summary: Internal POS data shows a similar Chanderi silk product underperformed with 38% sell-through and 18% return rate, primarily due to durability complaints. Meesho success is for an imitation at ₹349, not real silk. The premium demand may not translate to our value-fashion customer base, who may prefer the look without the maintenance burden.

nykaa.reason: Strong presence, full-price products, editorial backing, limited stock signal

myntra.reason: Strong presence, low discount, high review velocity, clean discount context

meesho.reason: Weak presence, imitation product, moderate rating, but provides value signal

internal.reason: Direct ground truth from similar SKU, clear underperformance data

chanderi-straight.bet_rationale: Premium signals are strong but internal POS contradicts with past failure. A small trial with a blended fabric (e.g., Chanderi-cotton) at ₹699-799 can test if durability was the key issue.
chanderi-straight.key_uncertainty: Will a blended Chanderi fabric at a lower price point overcome the durability complaints seen in the previous pure silk SKU?

Internal POS reorder rate for similar blended fabric trial exceeds 50%
Myntra rank drops below 20 or discount exceeds 40%
Nykaa editorial placement shifts away from Chanderi

chanderi-straight.demand_integrity: Mixed: Premium channels show clean demand, but internal data and Meesho imitation suggest value segment may not accept real silk

reasoning_trace.signal: No products found. Absent from premium D2C platform.
reasoning_trace.proves: Block-print cotton kurti is not a premium trend at Nykaa's price band.
reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Whether demand exists at value-fashion price points. Absence is neutral.
reasoning_trace.tension: No tension with other sources; absence is expected for a value-fashion item.

reasoning_trace.signal: 3 products found. Avg rank 12, avg rating 3.9, review velocity 101/mo. 2 of 3 have moderate discount (25-30%) with unclear driver.
reasoning_trace.proves: Organized mass retail has moderate demand for block-print cotton kurtis at ₹499-599.
reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Whether demand is style-driven or discount-driven. 2/3 products have discount dependency risk.
reasoning_trace.tension: Meesho shows strong volume at lower price; Myntra/Ajio demand may be price-sensitive.

reasoning_trace.signal: 1 product found: 15,000+ units sold, 340 resellers, 18% MoM reseller growth, ₹249 price point.
reasoning_trace.proves: Strong volume demand in tier-2/3/4 markets at ₹249. Reseller growth indicates organic pull.
reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Whether demand will sustain at higher price points (₹399-599). Price gap vs trend is -₹150.
reasoning_trace.tension: Meesho's strong volume at low price contrasts with Myntra/Ajio's moderate demand at higher price. Suggests price sensitivity.

reasoning_trace.signal: Similar past SKU (Jaipur Block Print Cotton Kurti) sold 78% sell-through in 210 stores, 44% margin, 4% returns, 34% repeat buy. Stockouts in 45 stores.
reasoning_trace.proves: Block-print cotton kurti is a proven winner for our base. Under-ordered last time.
reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Whether current designs will repeat success (design fatigue risk).
reasoning_trace.tension: Internal POS strongly supports demand, aligning with Meesho volume. Myntra/Ajio's discount dependency is a minor caution.

reasoning_trace.key_tensions: Meesho shows strong volume at ₹249, while Myntra/Ajio shows moderate demand at ₹499-599 with discount dependency. Internal POS confirms success at similar price band. Nykaa absence is neutral.
reasoning_trace.key_convergences: All sources (except Nykaa, which is absent) indicate demand for block-print cotton kurtis. Internal POS and Meesho both show strong volume signals. Myntra/Ajio adds organized retail validation.

blockprint-cotton.headline: All sources converge: Block-print cotton kurti is a proven value-fashion winner with strong volume signals from Meesho and internal POS, though Myntra/Ajio shows moderate discount dependency.

conflicts.title: Price sensitivity: Meesho strong at ₹249 vs Myntra/Ajio moderate at ₹499-599 with discount dependency
conflicts.myntra_says: Moderate demand at ₹499-599, but 2/3 products have discount dependency risk
conflicts.meesho_says: Strong volume at ₹249, reseller growth 18% MoM
conflicts.internal_says: Proven success at similar price band (₹399-599) with 78% sell-through
conflicts.buyer_question: Will demand sustain at ₹399-599 without heavy discounting, given Meesho's price point is ₹249?
conflicts.proves: Demand exists across price tiers, but price sensitivity is evident.
conflicts.cannot_prove: Whether the Myntra/Ajio demand is style-driven or discount-driven.

convergences.title: Strong volume signal from Meesho and internal POS
convergences.detail: Meesho sold 15,000+ units with 340 resellers and 18% MoM growth. Internal POS had 78% sell-through and stockouts in 45 stores. Both indicate robust demand.
convergences.proves: Block-print cotton kurti has proven demand in value-fashion and our core base.
convergences.cannot_prove: Whether current designs will match past performance (design fatigue).

convergences.title: Organized retail validation from Myntra/Ajio
convergences.detail: Myntra/Ajio shows 3 products with avg rank 12, avg rating 3.9, and review velocity 101/mo. One product (Biba) has clean demand signal with low discount.
convergences.proves: Demand extends to organized mass retail at ₹499-599.
convergences.cannot_prove: Whether the demand is sustainable without discounting for the other two products.

blockprint-cotton.upside_summary: Block-print cotton kurti is a proven category with strong volume from Meesho (15,000+ units) and internal POS (78% sell-through, stockouts). Organized retail validation from Myntra/Ajio adds confidence. Low returns (4%) and high repeat buy (34%) indicate customer satisfaction.
blockprint-cotton.catch_summary: Meesho's strong volume at ₹249 suggests price sensitivity; Myntra/Ajio demand may be partially discount-driven. Design fatigue is a risk given past success. Nykaa absence is neutral but indicates no premium pull.

nykaa.reason: Absent but expected for value-fashion; no data to evaluate quality.

myntra.reason: Moderate discount dependency on 2/3 products; review velocity adjusted downward.

meesho.reason: Strong volume, reseller growth, and clean price sensitivity flag.

internal.reason: High sell-through, low returns, repeat buy, and stockout evidence.

blockprint-cotton.bet_rationale: Internal POS and Meesho both show strong demand signals. Myntra/Ajio adds organized retail validation. The category is proven; the question is quantity, not whether to buy.
blockprint-cotton.key_uncertainty: Will current block-print designs avoid design fatigue and perform as well as the previous Jaipur Block Print Cotton Kurti?

reasoning_trace.tension: Myntra's ₹649 aligns with trend price band but conflicts with Nykaa's ₹1,490 and Meesho's ₹279

reasoning_trace.proves: Price-sensitive tier-2/3/4 demand is emerging and accelerating at ₹279
reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Whether this demand is style-driven or purely price-driven; quality of reviews uncertain
reasoning_trace.tension: Meesho's ₹279 is far below the trend price band (₹499-799) and Nykaa's ₹1,490, suggesting price sensitivity

reasoning_trace.proves: Bandhani has proven regional demand in Gujarat/Rajasthan but fails nationally
reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Whether a different design or price could succeed in other regions

reasoning_trace.key_convergences: All sources show Bandhani print demand exists; Myntra and Meesho both show clean demand signals; Internal POS confirms West India strength

bandhani-straight.headline: ⚡ Sources disagree: Bandhani print shows regional demand convergence in West India but price and geography conflicts remain

conflicts.title: Price point mismatch across sources
conflicts.buyer_question: Which price point reflects genuine demand vs. discount-driven sales?
conflicts.proves: Bandhani print has demand across price tiers, but the signal is fragmented
conflicts.cannot_prove: The optimal price point for a new launch

conflicts.title: Regional vs. national demand pattern
conflicts.internal_says: Strong in West India, weak in South/East
conflicts.buyer_question: Should this be a regional or national buy?
conflicts.proves: Bandhani demand is concentrated in Western India
conflicts.cannot_prove: Whether other regions will respond to a different design or price

conflicts.title: Nykaa premium presence vs. trend price band
conflicts.buyer_question: Is the premium Nykaa signal a leading indicator or an outlier?
conflicts.proves: Bandhani print has trickle-down potential from premium to mass
conflicts.cannot_prove: Whether the premium demand is sustainable or a one-off

convergences.title: Bandhani print demand exists across channels
convergences.detail: All four sources show positive signals: Nykaa (moderate), Myntra (clean), Meesho (accelerating), Internal POS (regional success)
convergences.proves: Bandhani print is a genuine trend with broad appeal
convergences.cannot_prove: The depth and geography of demand

convergences.title: Clean demand signal from Myntra and Meesho
convergences.detail: Myntra product is Strategic Value Pricing (low discount, good rating); Meesho shows accelerating reseller growth and high units sold
convergences.proves: Demand is not purely discount-driven at these price points
convergences.cannot_prove: Whether demand is style-driven or price-driven at Meesho's ₹279

convergences.detail: Past SKU sold well in Ahmedabad, Surat, Jaipur; Meesho shows demand in Gujarat, Rajasthan, Maharashtra
convergences.proves: Bandhani print has proven demand in Western India
convergences.cannot_prove: Whether demand extends to other regions

bandhani-straight.upside_summary: Bandhani print shows clean demand signals across premium (Nykaa), mass (Myntra), and value (Meesho) channels. Meesho's accelerating reseller growth (+35% MoM) and 4,200+ units sold indicate emerging ground-level demand. Internal POS confirms strong regional performance in West India, suggesting a proven base to build on.
bandhani-straight.catch_summary: Price points vary wildly (₹279 to ₹1,490), creating uncertainty about the optimal launch price. Internal POS data shows Bandhani is a regional bet, not national — past national rollout underperformed in South and East India. Nykaa's premium signal is weak (only 1 product, no full-price sales).

nykaa.reason: Only 1 product found, no full-price signal, moderate rating

myntra.reason: Clean Strategic Value Pricing, good rating, moderate rank

meesho.reason: High units sold, accelerating reseller growth, good rating

internal.reason: Real sales data with regional granularity, clear insights

bandhani-straight.bet_rationale: Bandhani print has proven regional demand in West India but fails nationally. Launch a small batch focused on Gujarat, Rajasthan, and Maharashtra at ₹599-799 to validate current demand, rather than a national rollout.
bandhani-straight.key_uncertainty: Will a modern colorway or design unlock demand in South and East India?

reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Cannot prove the trend is weak overall; only that premium D2C hasn't adopted it.
reasoning_trace.tension: Absent while Myntra/Ajio and Meesho show strong presence — suggests trend is mass-market, not premium.

reasoning_trace.signal: 2 products found, both Genuine Volume Driver but Paid Visibility. Rank 2 & 4, high discount (35-40%), high review velocity.
reasoning_trace.proves: Strong demand at ₹799-849 with discount accelerating real trend.
reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Cannot prove demand is organic — Paid Visibility inflates rank. Discount may be masking true price sensitivity.
reasoning_trace.tension: Strong presence conflicts with Nykaa's absence. Discount context says genuine, but Paid Visibility reduces rank confidence.

reasoning_trace.signal: 28,000+ units sold, 520 resellers, 3.6★ rating, price ₹399. Reseller growth +12% but not accelerating.
reasoning_trace.proves: Strong volume in tier-2/3/4 markets at low price point. Reseller count confirms grassroots demand.
reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Cannot prove demand is style-driven vs price-driven. Rating 3.6 is mediocre. No acceleration in reseller growth.
reasoning_trace.tension: Strong volume aligns with Myntra/Ajio but at much lower price. Nykaa absent — trend is value-driven.

reasoning_trace.signal: Past similar SKU sold 82% in 200 stores, stockouts in 68 stores, 38% margin, 6% returns. Buyer note: set format is key.
reasoning_trace.proves: Proven top-seller for our base. Set format drives value perception. Repeat buy rate 41%.
reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Cannot prove next season's designs will sustain velocity. Design fatigue possible.
reasoning_trace.tension: Internal success contradicts any notion of saturation. External signals show high volume but at lower prices — our base pays more for the set.

reasoning_trace.key_tensions: Nykaa absent vs Myntra/Ajio and Meesho strong. Myntra/Ajio demand is genuine but Paid Visibility inflates rank. Meesho volume is high but rating mediocre and not accelerating. Internal POS shows strong past success but at higher price point than Meesho.
reasoning_trace.key_convergences: All external sources (Myntra/Ajio, Meesho) show strong demand signals. Internal POS confirms the set format works. Discount context on Myntra/Ajio is Genuine Volume Driver.

fusion-palazzo.headline: ⚡ Sources disagree: Nykaa absent while mass retail and Meesho show strong demand; internal POS confirms past success but at higher price point.

conflicts.title: Nykaa absent vs Myntra/Ajio and Meesho strong
conflicts.nykaa_says: No premium demand for this trend.
conflicts.myntra_says: Strong demand at ₹799-849 with genuine volume driver.
conflicts.meesho_says: Very strong volume at ₹399 in tier-2/3/4.
conflicts.internal_says: Past success at ₹599-999 price band (set format).
conflicts.buyer_question: Is this trend only viable in mass/value channels, or can it be positioned at premium with the set format?
conflicts.proves: Trend has strong mass-market appeal but no premium D2C traction.
conflicts.cannot_prove: Cannot prove whether premium positioning would work with different branding or design.

conflicts.title: Myntra/Ajio demand is genuine but Paid Visibility inflates rank
conflicts.myntra_says: Rank 2 & 4, high review velocity, Genuine Volume Driver.
conflicts.meesho_says: High volume but lower rating (3.6) and no acceleration.
conflicts.internal_says: Past SKU was top seller without heavy discounting (12% markdown).
conflicts.buyer_question: How much of Myntra/Ajio demand is driven by ad spend vs organic preference?
conflicts.proves: Discount is accelerating real demand, but rank is inflated by paid visibility.
conflicts.cannot_prove: Cannot prove organic rank without paid support.

conflicts.title: Meesho volume high but rating mediocre and not accelerating
conflicts.meesho_says: 3.6★ rating, 28k units, but reseller growth +12% not accelerating.
conflicts.internal_says: Past SKU had 6% returns and 41% repeat buy — strong satisfaction.
conflicts.buyer_question: Is the Meesho demand price-driven (₹399) rather than style-driven, leading to lower satisfaction?
conflicts.proves: Volume exists but quality perception may be lower in value channel.
conflicts.cannot_prove: Cannot prove whether Meesho customers are satisfied or just price-sensitive.

convergences.title: Strong mass-market demand across channels
convergences.detail: Myntra/Ajio and Meesho both show high volume. Internal POS confirms set format works at ₹599-999.
convergences.proves: The fusion kurti+palazzo set has proven demand in mass retail and value channels.
convergences.cannot_prove: Cannot prove demand at premium price points (₹800+) or in new designs.

convergences.title: Discount is a volume driver, not a substitute
convergences.detail: Myntra/Ajio products classified as Genuine Volume Driver. Meesho price is low but volume is real.
convergences.proves: Discount is accelerating a real trend, not masking weak demand.
convergences.cannot_prove: Cannot prove demand would hold without discount at higher price points.

fusion-palazzo.upside_summary: The fusion kurti+palazzo set has strong multi-channel demand: Myntra/Ajio show high review velocity, Meesho shows massive volume, and your internal POS confirms the set format is a proven top-seller with stockouts. The trend is genuine and discount is accelerating real demand.
fusion-palazzo.catch_summary: Nykaa's absence suggests no premium D2C traction. Myntra/Ajio demand is partly paid visibility. Meesho's rating is mediocre and growth is not accelerating. Your past success was at a higher price point — design fatigue or saturation could emerge.

nykaa.reason: No data — absent source provides no signal.

myntra.reason: Strong signal but Paid Visibility reduces rank confidence.

meesho.reason: High volume, many resellers, clear regional spread.

internal.reason: High sell-through, stockouts, strong repeat buy rate.

fusion-palazzo.bet_rationale: Multiple clean signals converge on strong mass-market demand, and internal POS confirms the set format works. Despite Nykaa's absence and paid visibility noise, the volume and proven internal success justify widening to more stores.
fusion-palazzo.key_uncertainty: Will the next season's designs sustain the same velocity, or will design fatigue set in?

reasoning_trace.key_convergences: All sources show low discount (0-5%) indicating genuine demand, not discount-driven. All show good ratings (4.3-4.7★). Ajrakh print is present across premium, mass, and value channels, suggesting broad appeal.

ajrakh-cotton.headline: All sources converge on clean demand signals but volume is low across the board; early-stage trend with potential

convergences.title: Low discount across all channels
convergences.detail: Nykaa (5% off), Myntra (0% off), Meesho (no discount mentioned) all show minimal discounting, indicating demand is genuine and not price-propped.
convergences.cannot_prove: Volume is still low; cannot prove scalability

convergences.title: Consistent high ratings
convergences.detail: Nykaa 4.7★, Myntra 4.6★, Meesho 4.3★ — all above 4.0, indicating customer satisfaction across price tiers.
convergences.proves: Product quality and style are well-received
convergences.cannot_prove: Review counts are low (42-120); cannot prove broad consensus

convergences.title: Presence across premium, mass, and value channels
convergences.detail: Ajrakh print appears on Nykaa (premium), Myntra (mass), and Meesho (value), indicating multi-tier appeal.
convergences.proves: Style has potential to cross price segments
convergences.cannot_prove: Volume is low in each channel; cannot prove strong demand in any segment

ajrakh-cotton.upside_summary: Ajrakh print cotton kurti shows clean demand signals across premium, mass, and value channels with minimal discounting. High ratings and accelerating reseller growth on Meesho suggest early traction in tier-2/3/4 markets. The style has trickle-down potential from Nykaa's ₹1,250 price point to the ₹499-799 band.
ajrakh-cotton.catch_summary: Volume is low across all sources — Nykaa has only 65 reviews, Myntra rank 55, Meesho 480 units. The trend is still nascent and unproven at scale. Internal POS has no prior data, so we lack ground truth. The large price gap between Nykaa (₹1,250) and Meesho (₹299) raises questions about whether demand is style-driven or price-driven at lower price points.

nykaa.reason: Emerging presence with only 1 product; low volume but clean signal

myntra.reason: Weak presence with 1 product at rank 55; low review velocity

meesho.reason: Weak volume (480 units) but accelerating reseller growth; early signal

internal.reason: No prior data; cannot confirm or contradict

ajrakh-cotton.bet_rationale: All sources show clean demand with low discount, but volume is too low to justify a deeper buy. A small trial in representative stores will validate real demand before committing more capital.
ajrakh-cotton.key_uncertainty: Will the style sustain demand at ₹499-799 when scaled, or is current demand a niche early-adopter phenomenon?
watch_triggers: Meesho reseller count exceeds 50 or units sold > 2,000
Myntra rank improves to top 20 or review velocity > 50/month
Nykaa adds more Ajrakh products or stock pressure increases
Internal POS trial shows sell-through > 60% in first 4 weeks
missing_evidence: Internal POS sell-through data for Ajrakh print
Google search trend data for 'Ajrakh print kurti'
Competitor pricing and assortment depth in ₹499-799 band
ajrakh-cotton.demand_integrity: CLEAN — STRATEGIC PRICING: All products have low discount and good ratings. No noise flags detected.

reasoning_trace.signal: 1 product at ₹1,890 with 15% discount, 4.3★, 180 reviews, featured in wedding edit
reasoning_trace.proves: Premium demand exists for organza embroidered kurtis at ₹1,890+
reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Whether demand is sustainable at lower price points or beyond wedding season
reasoning_trace.tension: Discount (15%) contradicts 'zero-discount' expectation; premium-only signal conflicts with Meesho's weak presence

reasoning_trace.signal: 2 products at ₹1,199-1,299 with 10% discount, avg rank 13.5, 4.45★, 47 reviews/month
reasoning_trace.proves: Clean demand at ₹1,199-1,299 with low discount; strategic pricing
reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Whether demand is style-driven or price-driven; limited to Biba brand
reasoning_trace.tension: Strong signal at higher price band conflicts with Meesho's weak signal at ₹449

reasoning_trace.signal: 1 product at ₹449, 950+ units sold, 28 resellers, 3.5★, 310 reviews, +5% reseller growth
reasoning_trace.proves: Nascent demand at value price point in tier-2/3 regions
reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Whether demand is accelerating or sustainable; low rating suggests quality issues
reasoning_trace.tension: Weak volume and low rating conflict with strong signals from Nykaa and Myntra

reasoning_trace.signal: Similar SKU sold 71% in 120 stores, 48% margin, 5% markdown, 3-week window
reasoning_trace.proves: Organza festive kurtis are a proven 3-week play with strong margins
reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Whether the window will expand or repeat this year
reasoning_trace.tension: Confirms demand but warns of narrow window; conflicts with Nykaa's year-round editorial placement

reasoning_trace.key_tensions: Nykaa/Myntra show strong premium demand but Meesho weak at value; Internal POS confirms demand but only in a 3-week window
reasoning_trace.key_convergences: All sources agree organza embroidered kurtis have demand; Myntra and Internal POS both show clean signals with low discount

organza-embroidered.headline: ⚡ Sources disagree: Premium demand strong but value-market weak; internal data warns of narrow festive window

conflicts.title: Premium vs value demand gap
conflicts.meesho_says: Weak demand at ₹449 with low rating
conflicts.internal_says: Proven demand at ₹799-1,299 but only in festive window
conflicts.buyer_question: Will the trend trickle down to value-fashion or remain premium-only?
conflicts.proves: Premium demand is real; value demand is unproven
conflicts.cannot_prove: Whether value demand will emerge or is absent

conflicts.title: Timing window conflict
conflicts.nykaa_says: Editorial placement suggests year-round wedding season
conflicts.internal_says: Demand is a 3-week festive window only
conflicts.buyer_question: Is this a year-round trend or a narrow festive play?
conflicts.proves: Internal data proves narrow window; Nykaa suggests broader
conflicts.cannot_prove: Whether Nykaa's editorial is aspirational or reflects actual demand

convergences.title: Organza embroidered kurtis have demand at premium price points
convergences.detail: Nykaa and Myntra both show strong signals with low discount and good ratings
convergences.cannot_prove: Demand at lower price points or outside festive season

convergences.title: Low discount environment indicates genuine demand
convergences.detail: Myntra products at 10% off and Nykaa at 15% off; no deep discounting
convergences.cannot_prove: Whether demand is sustainable at full price

organza-embroidered.upside_summary: Organza embroidered kurtis show strong clean demand at premium price points (₹1,199-1,890) with low discounting, indicating genuine style preference. Internal POS confirms a similar SKU was a high-margin success in a festive window. The trend has editorial backing and brand support.
organza-embroidered.catch_summary: Value-market demand is weak and unproven (Meesho shows low volume and rating). Internal data warns the demand window is only 3 weeks, not year-round. Nykaa's premium signal may not trickle down to the ₹799-1,299 band. Timing risk is high.

nykaa.reason: Only 1 product found; discount present; editorial placement may inflate signal

myntra.reason: 2 products with clean strategic pricing, high ratings, and no noise flags

meesho.reason: Weak volume, low rating, and only 1 product; reseller growth not accelerating

internal.reason: High sell-through, strong margin, and clear timing insight from past data

organza-embroidered.bet_rationale: Premium demand is validated but value-market is unproven and timing is narrow. A small batch in representative stores during the festive window can test the trickle-down thesis without over-committing.
organza-embroidered.key_uncertainty: Will the trend gain traction at ₹799-1,299 in tier-2/3 markets, or remain a premium-only festive play?
watch_triggers: Meesho reseller growth accelerates above +10% MoM
Myntra rank drops below 20 or discount deepens beyond 30%
Nykaa adds more products or editorial features at lower price points
Internal POS early sell-through in first 2 weeks exceeds 50%
missing_evidence: Nykaa full-price product data (all discounted)
Meesho review velocity and repeat purchase data
Competitor pricing at ₹799-1,299 from other platforms
organza-embroidered.demand_integrity: Clean at premium; weak at value; timing risk from internal data

reasoning_trace.signal: 1 product at ₹1,795 with 20% discount, 4.2★, 150 reviews, featured in editorial edit.
reasoning_trace.proves: Premium D2C demand is emerging for linen straight kurti with mandarin collar at ₹1,795.
reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Whether this demand is sustainable or will trickle down to value-fashion price points.
reasoning_trace.tension: Nykaa's premium signal contrasts with Meesho's weak volume and Internal POS's failure with linen.

reasoning_trace.signal: 2 products at ₹799 with 15% discount, ranks 25-32, 3.8-3.9★, 78-89 reviews, moderate discount unclear driver.
reasoning_trace.proves: Organized mass retail shows early/weak demand at ₹799 with low discount dependency.
reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Whether demand is genuine style preference or driven by moderate discount.
reasoning_trace.tension: Moderate discount and unclear driver flag caution; aligns with Nykaa's emerging signal but conflicts with Internal POS's past failure.

reasoning_trace.signal: 1 product at ₹399, 220+ units sold, 3.2★, 12 resellers, +3% growth, not accelerating.
reasoning_trace.proves: Price-sensitive mass market shows weak volume signal with low ratings and slow growth.
reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Whether this is a genuine trend or a low-quality listing with limited appeal.
reasoning_trace.tension: Weak Meesho signal conflicts with Nykaa's premium presence and Myntra's moderate presence; suggests limited mass-market traction.

reasoning_trace.signal: Similar SKU (Linen Straight Kurti SS23) underperformed: 29% sell-through, 45% markdown, 24% returns, only worked in South Mumbai.
reasoning_trace.proves: Linen kurtis do not work for our value-fashion customer base at scale due to wrinkling and returns.
reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Whether a linen-blend (less wrinkling) at a lower price point could change the outcome.
reasoning_trace.tension: Strong conflict with external signals: Nykaa and Myntra show emerging demand, but Internal POS shows past failure with linen.

reasoning_trace.key_tensions: Internal POS directly contradicts external signals: linen kurtis failed in our stores (29% sell-through, 24% returns) while Nykaa and Myntra show emerging demand. Meesho weak volume suggests limited mass-market traction. Discount context on Myntra/Ajio is unclear driver.
reasoning_trace.key_convergences: Nykaa and Myntra/Ajio both show emerging demand for linen straight kurti with Chinese/mandarin collar at premium and mid-price points. No source shows strong, clean demand.

linen-chinese-collar.headline: ⚡ Sources disagree: Internal POS shows linen kurtis failed in our stores (29% sell-through, 24% returns), while Nykaa and Myntra show emerging demand. Meesho weak. Proceed with caution.

conflicts.title: Internal POS contradicts external demand signals
conflicts.nykaa_says: Emerging premium demand at ₹1,795 with editorial placement.
conflicts.myntra_says: Early/weak demand at ₹799 with moderate discount.
conflicts.meesho_says: Weak volume at ₹399 with low ratings.
conflicts.internal_says: Linen kurtis underperformed: 29% sell-through, 45% markdown, 24% returns. Our customer doesn't iron.
conflicts.buyer_question: Can a linen-blend (less wrinkling) at a lower price point overcome the past failure with pure linen?
conflicts.proves: External signals show some demand for linen straight kurti, but our internal data proves linen kurtis failed in our value-fashion stores.
conflicts.cannot_prove: Whether the current trend is different (linen-blend, lower price) or will repeat the same failure.

conflicts.title: Meesho weak vs Nykaa/Myntra moderate
conflicts.nykaa_says: Emerging premium presence with editorial backing.
conflicts.myntra_says: Moderate presence with early/weak demand signal.
conflicts.meesho_says: Weak volume (220 units), low rating (3.2), slow reseller growth (+3%).
conflicts.internal_says: No direct Meesho data, but past linen failure suggests limited mass appeal.
conflicts.buyer_question: Is the trend limited to premium/urban customers, or will it eventually reach mass market?
conflicts.proves: Demand is concentrated in premium and organized retail, not yet in price-sensitive mass market.
conflicts.cannot_prove: Whether Meesho will accelerate or remain weak.

convergences.title: Emerging demand for linen straight kurti with collar
convergences.detail: Nykaa (1 product, editorial featured) and Myntra/Ajio (2 products, ranks 25-32) both show emerging demand for this silhouette at premium and mid-price points.
convergences.proves: There is some consumer interest in linen straight kurti with Chinese/mandarin collar.
convergences.cannot_prove: Whether this demand is sustainable, price-sensitive, or will translate to our value-fashion customer base.

linen-chinese-collar.upside_summary: Nykaa and Myntra/Ajio show emerging demand for linen straight kurti with Chinese collar, suggesting a potential trend in premium and organized retail. The silhouette is workwear-friendly and could appeal to urban professionals. If a linen-blend version reduces wrinkling, it might overcome past issues.
linen-chinese-collar.catch_summary: Internal POS data is a major red flag: a similar linen kurti underperformed with 29% sell-through, 45% markdown, and 24% returns. Our customer base prioritizes low-maintenance fabrics. Meesho shows weak volume, indicating limited mass-market traction. Discount context on Myntra/Ajio is unclear driver.

nykaa.reason: Only 1 product found, moderate signal strength, no full-price products.

myntra.reason: 2 products with moderate discount and unclear driver; review velocity down-weighted 25%.

meesho.reason: Weak volume, low rating, slow growth; data point-in-time.

internal.reason: Direct sales data from 60 stores with clear outcome and buyer note.

linen-chinese-collar.bet_rationale: External signals show emerging demand, but internal data strongly warns against pure linen. Test a small batch of linen-blend kurtis (lower price, less wrinkling) in a few stores to validate if the fabric issue is resolved.
linen-chinese-collar.key_uncertainty: Will a linen-blend version at ₹699-999 reduce returns and improve sell-through compared to the pure linen failure?
watch_triggers: Meesho reseller growth accelerates above +10% MoM
Myntra/Ajio rank improves to top 10 with higher review velocity
Nykaa adds more products or full-price listings
Internal POS test batch shows sell-through above 50% with return rate below 10%
missing_evidence: Linen-blend specific product data (most listings are pure linen)
Customer feedback on wrinkling for linen-blend vs pure linen
Regional demand data beyond 3 regions on Meesho
linen-chinese-collar.demand_integrity: Needs Closer Watch — external signals are early and mixed; internal data contradicts strongly.
